generation_mode_record.draft_source: deterministic fixture record, not a model run
generation_mode_record.acceptance_predicate: quality gate passes, baseline refs exist, residuals are recorded, and promotion remains experimental
generation_mode_record.wall_clock_time: measured in local validation command; no fixed timing claim
generation_mode_record.quality_or_pass_result: pass: quality metric present and residual boundary recorded
generation_mode_record.repair_or_fallback: fallback path available through baseline autoregressive mode if candidate mode fails
metric_definitions: useful_solution_per_second: accepted deterministic fixture per local command duration
quality: schema and semantic acceptance predicates pass
non_claims: Does not promote any Appendix C support state.
Does not prove model quality, deployed generation speed, or runtime behavior.

resource_budget_record.value_hypothesis: A generation-mode record is useful only if quality, residuals, and baseline refs travel with latency accounting.
cost_estimate: wall-clock command duration is recorded in the result file, not in a support-state claim
displaced_costs: no model inference, no user-facing deployment, no benchmark suite
resource_budget_record.quality_predicate: quality metric and residual metric are both present before any experimental acceptance
resource_budget_record.escalation_rule: Escalate if a record tries to promote from latency alone or omits baseline refs.
residuals: Local deterministic fixture only; no model-quality evidence.

non_claims: Does not promote any support state.
Does not prove fast generation model behavior.
